description: Completeness ledger. Provenance proves emitted-code <= IR op (origin). This ledger proves COMPLETENESS: every claimed behavioral capability maps to the IR op(s) that implement it AND at least one conformance fixture that exercises it for real. A capability with no real fixture is unverifiable and must not be claimed. scripts/axir-ledger-check.mjs fails the build if any listed op or fixture is missing. This is the structural answer to why a non-functional agent() shipped: provenance could not see behavior that was absent from both the IR and the packages, and no gate required a real-execution fixture.

entries.summary: The agent executor/responder RLM instruction is rendered from IR into agent state (not empty).
entries.state: red-until-port: executor_description is not yet rendered by @agent_factory

entries.summary: A real embedded engine executes model-authored code end-to-end through forward() and produces the completion -- NOT a ScriptedCodeRuntime returning canned payloads.
entries.state: VERIFIED IN ALL FIVE LANGUAGES: go (goja), rust (quickjs feature), cpp (quickjs profile -DAX_CONFORMANCE_QUICKJS), python (in-process quickjs wheel), java (quickjs4j/chicory via reflection). Each runs the full forward() loop through a real engine executing model-authored final() code. Engines are optional deps, so agent_runtime_real runs in engine-enabled build lanes, not the default conformance suite.

entries.summary: Across multiple distiller and executor turns, each turn's request carries the accumulated per-turn feedback -- the Action Log and the refreshed liveRuntimeState snapshot of live session variables -- so the model sees its own prior work and converges to final(), instead of re-exploring blindly until max_actor_steps. NOT a 1-2-turn canned case.
entries.gate: G1 real-engine antidote + G6 response perturbation
entries.state: VERIFIED via a real-engine fixture driving a 2-turn distiller and a 2-turn executor: the later request must contain a marker produced by executing the earlier turn. distillProbe7731 can only reach the distiller's 2nd request through the newly-provided distiller actionLog/liveRuntimeState (@build_distiller_inputs); 'Current runtime state:' + execLiveStateProbe8842 can only reach the executor's 2nd request when @agent_runtime_refresh_state_summary repopulates runtime_session_state each non-error turn. Proven to FAIL without the fix (distiller request was input/context-only and liveRuntimeState rendered empty) and anti-hardcoded by the response-perturbation gate (mutating the probe responses drops the markers and fails the runner).

entries.summary: Actor code can await the built-in llmQuery([{query, context}]) primitive: each item runs a focused sub-query through a dedicated AxGen (task:string, context:json -> answer:string) against the same client, and the answers return to the runtime as a real string[] the model indexes -- NOT a stub or canned value.
entries.gate: G1 real-engine antidote + G6 response perturbation
entries.state: VERIFIED IN ALL FIVE LANGUAGES via a real-engine fixture: the executor awaits llmQuery([{query:'llmQueryTaskProbe5521', context:{detail:'contextMarker8843'}}]) (both markers must appear in the sub-query request, proving the sub-gen ran with the passed task+context), captures the result, then finals with answer:'EXTRACTED_'+result[0]. 'EXTRACTED_subAnswerMarker9931' can only reach the responder request when the sub-query consumed the scripted answer AND the returned value is a JS array the model indexes with [0] -- which exposed and drove a Go fix (AxArray.MarshalJSON, so the wrapper struct serializes as a JSON array across the host-callable boundary) and an rquickjs fix (a host callable may claim an agent-declared reserved primitive name like llmQuery; only runtime-installed built-ins conflict). Each language registers llmQuery through a package-neutral seam (Go runtimeCallableRegistrar + goja RegisterHostCallable; Rust AxCodeRuntime::register_host_callable trait method + CoreHost::register_runtime_callable; Java AxCodeRuntime.HostCallable default method; C++ AxCodeRuntime::register_host_callable virtual recovered via code_runtime_registry; Python register_callable). Anti-hardcoded by the response-perturbation gate (mutating the sub-query answer or the executor code drops the marker and fails the runner).

entries.summary: Audio-typed input fields are transcribed to text via the AI client before the agent loop, and the transcribed text reaches the distiller/executor request -- NOT a canned string.
entries.state: VERIFIED IN ALL FIVE LANGUAGES. Required converting rust's chat-only scoped client bridge to a method-dispatch closure FnMut(&str,Value) so chat+transcribe share one &mut borrow; transcribe added to the rust AxAIClient trait + cpp base AIClient + java AiClient interface. The new intrinsic.agent.transcribe is bound in all four explicit host-name maps and drift-guarded by TestAgentCrossLanguageParity.

entries.summary: When summarizerOptions is set, a pending checkpoint is summarized by a real model call (intrinsic.ai.complete_once) and the model's summary replaces the deterministic fallback -- NOT a canned value. Deterministic fallback is retained when the flag is off.
entries.state: VERIFIED IN ALL FIVE LANGUAGES. Uses the op-callable raw-chat intrinsic intrinsic.ai.complete_once (already bridged in all five incl. rust's scoped closure), so no new per-language host code. restore_runtime_state handling was added to all five agent_forward conformance runners to pre-set the pending checkpoint. The fixture asserts expected_exported_state_subset.checkpoint_state.summary == the model response AND summary_source==model, so mutating the model response fails the test (anti-hardcode verified: an impl that ignored the model output or hardcoded a summary would not pass).

entries.summary: The cross-run context map is read (injected into the distiller's context + the distiller prompt's hasContextMap section) AND evolved by a full TS-parity two-stage pipeline: a context-map distiller (itemTags + cacheCandidates) then a cartographer (ADD/DELETE/REPLACE operations), both via intrinsic.ai.complete_once. Operations apply to parsed [id] items across 6 fixed sections; helpful/harmful/stale tags adjust per-item scores; score-based eviction trims the map to maxChars; evolution is gated by infiniteEvolve || steps<evolveSteps. The evolved map persists through export/restore. Distiller scores AND cartographer operations are both asserted from the model, not canned.
entries.state: VERIFIED IN ALL FIVE LANGUAGES (full TS parity). Create: @agent_factory builds state.context_map from options.contextMap (map string/snapshot + maxChars/infiniteEvolve/evolveSteps/scores/next_id) -- agent-context-map-config uses a FRESH agent (no restore), the cartographer ADDs an item, and the exported text+scores+steps are asserted. Read: agent-context-map-read restores a non-evolving map (infiniteEvolve:false, evolveSteps:0) and asserts the map text reaches the DISTILLER request, isolating read from evolve. Evolve: agent-context-map-evolve asserts the distiller itemTags update scores AND the cartographer ADD operation mints a new [id] item in the rendered text (mutating either model response fails the test). Evict: agent-context-map-evict tags an item harmful and asserts it is dropped under a tight maxChars while the neutral item survives (flipping the tag to helpful fails the test). next_id is a persisted counter (no numeric id parsing -- rust mul rejects string operands, so ids are minted from the counter and eviction tie-breaks on text order). context_map is part of the export/restore state contract.

entries.summary: When contextPolicy.tombstoning is enabled, a resolved-error action-log entry (error turn followed by a success turn) gets its tombstone written by a real model call (intrinsic.ai.complete_once with the TS TOMBSTONE_SUMMARIZER_DESCRIPTION) -- a one-line [TOMBSTONE]: summary -- replacing the deterministic fallback, with tombstone_source=model. Also wires @agent_apply_context_management into the live forward loop (previously it ran only in the fixture op), so deterministic tombstones + error-pruning now run during forward().
entries.state: VERIFIED IN ALL FIVE LANGUAGES. agent-tombstone-llm restores an action_log with an error entry (tags:[error]) followed by a success entry, sets options.tombstoning:true, and asserts the exported action_log[0].tombstone == the model response with tombstone_source==model (the action-log sanitizer was extended to preserve tombstone_source). Mutating the model response fails the test (anti-hardcode verified). Mirrors the checkpoint summarizer pattern (llm_pending/llm_input set in @agent_apply_context_management, consumed by @agent_apply_llm_tombstone_summary post-loop).

entries.summary: An AxFlow node executes its program by invoking the model through intrinsic.agent.stage_forward and writes the result into flow state (as {name}Result + merged fields); the final state is projected via the returns spec. Nested flows recurse through the same path.
entries.state: VERIFIED IN ALL FIVE LANGUAGES. simple-forward-returns runs a single node whose scripted response {answer:Paris} becomes the flow output (output depends on the model response, not a canned value); nested-flow-program-call proves a nested flow's inner node executes a real stage_forward (chat-log entry 'nested.inner').

entries.summary: AxFlow control steps execute deterministically in all five: branch routes to the matching when-branch by predicate/value; while loops until the condition fails (max-iteration guarded); feedback re-runs a labelled body until its condition fails. All dispatched in @flow_execute_step.
entries.state: VERIFIED IN ALL FIVE LANGUAGES. Each fixture asserts the state mutations produced by the chosen control path (branch taken, loop iteration count, feedback re-entry) match the TS-derived golden.

entries.summary: AxFlow plans parallel groups from barrier flags + read/write dependency analysis (@flow_plan) and executes independent steps as a group, merging their results (@flow_merge_parallel_results / parallelMerge). Auto-parallel is controllable per flow + per forward.
entries.state: VERIFIED IN ALL FIVE LANGUAGES. plan-barriers asserts the computed parallel groups/maxParallelism; the execution fixtures assert both independent results are produced and merged into the final state.

entries.summary: AxFlow derive applies a per-item transform over an array input field (or once over a scalar) and writes the mapped result to the output field — NOT a single model call over the whole state. @flow_execute_step iterates the input list and calls the transform per item via intrinsic.object.call_method.
entries.state: VERIFIED IN ALL FIVE LANGUAGES. derive-per-item maps an uppercase transform over ["ada","grace"] -> ["ADA","GRACE"] (per-item) and a scalar "lin" -> "LIN", with expected_request_count:0 (no model call). FIXES a prior gap where derive routed through stage_forward over the whole state instead of per-item (TS flow.ts:1321-1360).

entries.summary: AxFlow forward consults an optional cacheStore: on a key hit it returns the cached output and skips execution; on a miss it runs and writes the output (errors swallowed). Key is built from the input (@flow_cache_key). NOTE: the key uses json.stable_stringify, NOT the TS SHA256 — keys diverge between the TS build and generated packages, so a cache must not be shared across them (documented simplification).
entries.state: VERIFIED IN ALL FIVE LANGUAGES. cache-hit asserts execution is skipped (request count 0) and the cached output returned; cache-miss asserts the output is written. KNOWN MINOR GAPS (not ported, documented here): TS SHA256 cache-key (IR uses json.stable_stringify); streamingForward (TS yields one accumulated result); nodeExtended; multi-source abort-signal merge; finer step-level execution logging.

entries.summary: A transient Anthropic error event is classified by error.type to its HTTP status (overloaded_error->529, api_error->500, rate_limit_error->429, client errors->4xx, authentication_error->auth, unknown->refusal) in both the non-streaming and streaming parsers. A pre-content streaming transient error (e.g. Anthropic's HTTP-200 overloaded_error SSE event) is retried with the same exponential backoff apiCall applies to a 529 before surfacing; the balancer then fails over on the surfaced retryable status (529 is in every balancer's retryable set). TS peekStreamForFailover + mid-stream failure recording are workarounds for TS's lazy ReadableStream (errors defer past the balancer try/catch); the eager/synchronous ports have no portable equivalent and instead fail over through the chat-routed balancer loop, proven by balancer-status-529-stream-failover.
entries.state: VERIFIED IN ALL FIVE LANGUAGES via npm run test:axir. Anthropic error fixtures assert error.type -> error class + HTTP status (overloaded->Status 529, rate_limit->429, api->500, invalid_request->400, authentication->Auth, unknown->Refusal) on both chat and stream paths. anthropic-stream-retry-overloaded proves a first-delta overloaded_error is retried then the next response's content is returned; anthropic-stream-retry-exhausted proves the 529 surfaces once the retry budget is spent; anthropic-stream-error-overloaded opts out of retries (maxRetries:0) to assert the classification in isolation. balancer-status-529-failover and -stream-failover prove the balancer fails over to a healthy backup on a retryable 529 via both chat() and stream(). peekStreamForFailover/mid-stream recording intentionally not ported (TS-lazy-streaming-specific; the ports stream synchronously).

entries.summary: A distiller respond(task, evidence) completion skips the executor stage entirely: @agent_forward re-types the payload to final and runs the responder directly (zero executor stage calls), guarded by directResponse!='off'. Static agents (no callables) render a respond-only distiller prompt (final hidden via disabledBy:directRespondOnly); agents with callables render respond alongside final under the Direct Response covenant (directRespondMode). Executor prompts never see the direct-respond flags (stage-scoped flag clone). Executor-phase respond() aliases to final. Real engines install the respond global producing {type:'respond', args}.
entries.gate: G2 anti-facade self-test + cross-target coverage + G1 real-engine
entries.state: VERIFIED IN ALL FIVE LANGUAGES. direct-respond-skip asserts expected_request_count:2 with the evidence value materialized in the responder request and no executor-shaped request; direct-respond-covenant-with-functions asserts the dynamic prompt (covenant section + both completion primitives) with the executor still running on final(); direct-respond-kill-switch asserts directResponse:off removes respond from the prompt and the final flow is unchanged. agent-runtime-real-javascript-respond executes MODEL-AUTHORED respond() code through the real engine (goja verified in-repo; other engines follow the real-final fixture lanes) and asserts the two-request skip. TS parity: agent.directRespond.test.ts (20 cases incl. shared/fallback sessions, cross-run state export, streaming/eval paths).

entries.summary: Nested record identifiers are collected into a citation chain, invalid citations trigger a retry, and hidden citations remain available in agent state without leaking into the typed answer.
entries.state: VERIFIED IN ALL FIVE LANGUAGES. The retry fixture rejects an invalid citation before accepting the corrected response; the hidden-surface fixture proves nested record ids populate exported citation state while the public answer stays clean.

entries.summary: Repeated runtime errors and dead ends become stable failure signals, and playbook coverage only suppresses signatures backed by retained or deliberately no-op curated feedback.
entries.state: VERIFIED IN ALL FIVE LANGUAGES. Runtime error and dead-end traces carry normalized signatures, while coverage distinguishes live, pruned, no-op, transient-failure, and legacy history states.

entries.summary: An agent playbook configuration can attach a seeded snapshot before the first forward call and inject its durable guidance into the live stage request.
entries.state: VERIFIED IN ALL FIVE LANGUAGES. A fresh configured agent renders the seeded failures_to_avoid rule into the request and returns the scripted typed answer.

entries.summary: setInstruction replaces the actor-stage instruction, addActorInstruction appends a durable addendum, and the composed text reaches the actor request without changing the responder contract.
entries.state: VERIFIED IN ALL FIVE LANGUAGES. Request assertions prove both explicit instruction fragments and attached playbook guidance are composed into live stage prompts.

entries.summary: ACE reflection normalizes scalar, string, object, and array bullet-tag shapes before feedback updates so malformed tag containers cannot corrupt a playbook.
entries.state: VERIFIED IN ALL FIVE LANGUAGES. Scalar object and string tag variants normalize deterministically before curator operations and artifact persistence.

entries.summary: Current adaptive Anthropic models, including qualified provider model ids, emit adaptive thinking with effort mapping and summarized or omitted display according to showThoughts.
entries.state: VERIFIED IN ALL FIVE LANGUAGES. Stable and qualified adaptive model ids select adaptive thinking; showThoughts false selects omitted display while the normal path uses summarized display.

entries.summary: Adaptive Anthropic requests omit temperature, top_p, and top_k while retaining thinking and output effort configuration.
entries.state: VERIFIED IN ALL FIVE LANGUAGES. Both direct and qualified adaptive model fixtures assert the sampling keys are absent from the provider JSON.

entries.summary: Agent playbook evolution evaluates a baseline, mines grounded weaknesses, applies a proposal, re-evaluates within a metric budget, accepts sufficient held-in gain, and restores the exact prior snapshot on rejection.
entries.state: VERIFIED IN ALL FIVE LANGUAGES. The shared fixtures assert accepted and rejected outcomes, metricCallsUsed, held-in values, byte-exact rollback with an exported-state subset, and metric_budget exhaustion before validation.
